text.title: Cloud Run Jobs Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the cloud-run-jobs methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Every parallel task reprocesses the full dataset; output is duplicated and bill scales by task_count.
antipattern.root-cause: Worker code reads the full input slice unconditionally — never reads `CLOUD_RUN_TASK_INDEX`.
antipattern.fix: Refactor the worker to slice work by `CLOUD_RUN_TASK_INDEX` / `CLOUD_RUN_TASK_COUNT`; add unit test on slice math.

antipattern.symptom: Retries cause duplicate writes / double charges; downstream state corrupts.
antipattern.root-cause: Retry of the same `CLOUD_RUN_TASK_INDEX` produces a different effect than the first execution.
antipattern.fix: Add idempotency keys to writes; OR set `--max-retries=0` and own retry policy externally.

antipattern.symptom: Long jobs silently truncate at 10 min default and emit opaque failure.
antipattern.root-cause: No `--task-timeout` set on `gcloud run jobs create`.
antipattern.fix: Pass `--task-timeout=<expected_max_duration_plus_buffer>` and surface the value in the deploy manifest.

antipattern.symptom: Default compute SA grants too-broad permissions to scheduled invocation.
antipattern.root-cause: Cloud Scheduler invoker is the default compute SA (visible in `gcloud scheduler jobs describe`).
antipattern.fix: Create dedicated SA, grant `roles/run.invoker` on the job only, point Scheduler at it.
